gbtmkit 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference
    • Getting started with gbtmkit
  • Changelog

Stage 2: select the number of groups

Source: R/select.R
select_n_groups.Rd

Fits the model for each candidate number of groups and picks the one with the lowest BIC. Each candidate uses a polynomial degree of degree for every group unless a per-candidate degrees list is supplied.

Usage

select_n_groups(
  spec,
  engine = gbtm_engines(),
  candidates = 2:6,
  degree = 3L,
  degrees = NULL,
  method = NULL,
  by = c("bic", "aic"),
  hessian = FALSE,
  itermax = 100L,
  seed = NULL,
  ...
)

Arguments

spec

A gbtm_spec.

engine

Engine name; see gbtm_engines().

candidates

Integer vector of group numbers to try (default 2:6).

degree

Single polynomial degree applied to every group (default 3, cubic). Ignored when degrees is supplied.

degrees

Optional list, one integer vector per candidate, for full control of the shape at each group number.

method

Estimation method to use (e.g. the winner of select_algorithm()).

by

Criterion to minimize, "bic" (default) or "aic".

hessian, itermax, seed, ...

Passed to gbtm_fit().

Value

A gbtm_selection object with $table, $best (the chosen number of groups), and the fitted models.

Examples

# \donttest{
data("sim_binary", package = "gbtmkit")
spec <- gbtm_spec(sim_binary, c("y1","y2","y3","y4"),
                  c("t1","t2","t3","t4"), id = "id", family = "binomial")
if (requireNamespace("trajeR", quietly = TRUE))
  select_n_groups(spec, candidates = 2:5, degree = 1)
#> Starting Values
#> 0.50.5-2.1690177846160201.218309550283050
#> 
#> 
#> Likelihood
#> initial  value 3454.147366 
#> iter   2 value 3423.436500
#> iter   3 value 3414.748985
